question: The experimental results do not ______ the theory proposed by the lead scientist.
explanation:
• **Bear out**: To confirm or support a statement. ✅
• **Bear up**: To remain brave in a difficult time. 💪
• **Bear with**: To be patient. ⏳
• **Bear down**: To push or move towards. 🚜

question: Don't worry about the rumors; they will eventually ______ if you stay silent.
explanation:
• **Blow over**: To pass without serious effect. 🌬️
• **Blow up**: To explode. 🧨
• **Blow out**: To extinguish. 🎂
• **Blow off**: To ignore or skip. 💨

question: It was a nearly impossible mission, but the special forces managed to ______.
explanation:
• **Bring off**: To succeed in something very difficult. 🏆
• **Bring about**: To cause change. 🌀
• **Bring up**: To raise or mention. 👶
• **Bring in**: To earn or introduce. 💵

question: The tragic news ______ a great deal of public sympathy for the victims.
explanation:
• **Called forth**: To evoke or elicit a reaction. 📣
• **Called on**: To visit. 🚪
• **Called off**: To cancel. ❌
• **Called in**: To summon assistance. 📞

question: The diplomat ______ the difficult negotiation with great skill and patience.
explanation:
• **Carried off**: To handle a difficult situation successfully. 🥇
• **Carried on**: To continue. ➡️
• **Carried out**: To execute a task. ⚙️
• **Carried away**: To lose control due to emotion. 🤩

question: I thought the plan was brilliant, but it didn't quite ______ in reality.
explanation:
• **Come off**: To succeed as intended. ⚙️
• **Come by**: To obtain. 🛍️
• **Come about**: To happen. 🌀
• **Come across**: To meet by chance. 🔍

question: The lawyers are meeting tomorrow to ______ the final sales agreement.
explanation:
• **Draw up**: To prepare a formal document. 📄
• **Draw in**: To attract or involve. 🧲
• **Draw out**: To prolong or make longer. 📏
• **Draw on**: To use a resource. 🏦

question: The crowd began to ______ the fighters, making the situation even more dangerous.
explanation:
• **Egg on**: To urge or incite someone (usually to do something bad). 🥚
• **Others**: These are not standard phrasal verbs. ❌

question: If our primary plan fails, we will have to ______ our emergency reserves.
explanation:
• **Fall back on**: To use as a last resort. 🛡️
• **Fall out with**: To argue. 😤
• **Fall through**: To fail. 📉
• **Fall behind**: To be slow. 🐢

question: The politician managed to ______ the hostile questions from the reporters.
explanation:
• **Fend off**: To defend against or deflect. 🤺
• **Fend for**: To take care of oneself (Fend for yourself). 🏠

question: I can't quite understand what you are trying to ______ with these remarks.
explanation:
• **Get at**: To imply or suggest something. 🎯
• **Get over**: To recover. ❤️‍🩹
• **Get by**: To survive. 💵
• **Get through**: To finish. 📞

question: The company tried to ______ the environmental damage it had caused.
explanation:
• **Gloss over**: To ignore or cover up a mistake. 🌫️
• **Others**: These are not standard phrasal verbs. ❌

question: You shouldn't ______ appearances; sometimes the best people look very ordinary.
explanation:
• **Go by**: To follow or judge by. ⏳
• **Go for**: To choose or attack. 🎯
• **Go through**: To examine. 📖
• **Go off**: To explode. ⏰

question: We need to have a meeting to ______ the differences in the contract.
explanation:
• **Iron out**: To resolve small problems. 👔
• **Others**: These are not standard phrasal verbs. ❌

question: She would ______ the chance to study at a prestigious university abroad.
explanation:
• **Jump at**: To accept an opportunity eagerly. 🤩
• **Jump on**: To criticize someone suddenly. 🗯️
• **Jump in**: To interrupt or join quickly. 🌊

question: The witness was accused of ______ vital information from the court.
explanation:
• **Keeping back**: To withhold information. ✋
• **Keeping on**: To continue. ➡️
• **Keeping up**: To maintain speed. 🏃
• **Keeping away**: To stay distant. 🚫

question: The constitution ______ the fundamental duties of every citizen.
explanation:
• **Lays down**: To establish rules or sacrifice. ⚖️
• **Lays off**: To dismiss workers. 💼
• **Lays out**: To spend or arrange. 💰
• **Lays in**: To store. 📦

question: The sequel to the movie failed to ______ the expectations of the fans.
explanation:
• **Live up to**: To fulfill expectations. 📈
• **Live on**: To survive on (food/money). 🍛
• **Live through**: To experience difficulty. 🕯️
• **Live out**: To spend the rest of life. 👴

question: The robbers ______ all the cash and jewels before the police arrived.
explanation:
• **Made away with**: To steal. 🏃‍♂️
• **Made out**: To understand. 👂
• **Made up**: To invent or reconcile. ✨
• **Made for**: To head towards. 🏃

question: He was so tired that he ______ right in the middle of the lecture.
explanation:
• **Nodded off**: To fall asleep unintentionally. 😴
• **Others**: These are not standard phrasal verbs for sleep. ❌

question: You will feel much better if you just ______ to your mistakes.
explanation:
• **Own up**: To confess or admit fault. 🗣️
• **Others**: These are not standard phrasal verbs. ❌

question: The government decided to ______ old currency notes over a period of six months.
explanation:
• **Phase out**: To gradually discontinue. 📉
• **Phase in**: To gradually introduce. 📈

question: No one thought he could win the election, but he managed to ______ it ______.
explanation:
• **Pull off**: To succeed in something difficult. 💪
• **Pull through**: To recover. 🏥
• **Pull up**: To stop. 🚗
• **Pull down**: To demolish. 🏗️

question: The doctor has ______ the possibility of a heart attack after the tests.
explanation:
• **Ruled out**: To exclude or eliminate. 🚫
• **Ruled over**: To govern. 👑

question: In his autobiography, he ______ his vision for the future of the country.
explanation:
• **Sets forth**: To explain or state clearly. 🗺️
• **Sets off**: To start a journey. ✈️
• **Sets in**: To begin (weather). 🌧️
• **Sets up**: To establish. 🏗️

question: The CEO was forced to ______ after the financial scandal became public.
explanation:
• **Step down**: To resign. 🚪
• **Step up**: To increase or take responsibility. 📈
• **Step in**: To intervene. 👮
• **Step out**: To leave briefly. 🚶

question: We need to ______ the details of the trip before we book the tickets.
explanation:
• **Talk over**: To discuss thoroughly. 🤝
• **Talk back**: To reply rudely. 😤
• **Talk down**: To make something seem less important. 📉

question: You should ______ the new car on a long drive before you decide to buy it.
explanation:
• **Try out**: To test or experiment. 🧪
• **Try on**: To test clothes for size. 👕

question: We had to ______ the storm in a small cabin in the woods.
explanation:
• **Wait out**: To wait until something unpleasant ends. 🕒
• **Wait on**: To serve. 🍽️
• **Wait for**: To await. ⏳

question: The rebels were forced to ______ the superior power of the national army.
explanation:
• **Yield to**: To surrender or give in to pressure. 🙇
